expectations.text: Output contains all 6 rounds (Round 1 through Round 6)
expectations.evidence: All 6 rounds present: Round 1 (line 63), Round 2 (line 95), Round 3 (line 113), Round 4 (line 131), Round 5 (line 143), Round 6 (line 164)

expectations.text: Optimizer, Frontline Operator, and COO personas all appear and are distinct
expectations.evidence: Optimizer proposes self-serve platform with cost analysis; Frontline Operator challenges with completion rates, CSM attrition, intervention model; COO synthesizes with pilot-first

expectations.text: Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)

expectations.evidence: Categories: capacity, quality-risk, people-impact, execution-risk, dependency, measurement

expectations.text: Round 4 references specific mitigations from Round 3, not just repeating Round 2
expectations.evidence: Round 4 attacks specific mitigations: 48-hour stall trigger creates interrupt-driven hellscape, CSM advisory competes with workload, enterprise specialist retraining underestimated, pilot criteria set to pass

expectations.text: Round 6 contains one of the 5 valid recommendations
A CEO Summary section exists that is standalone and uses business language
expectations.evidence: CEO Summary at lines 13-14, standalone with investment amount, customer impact, and approval requirements

expectations.text: Metrics table present with all 8 metrics filled in
expectations.evidence: All 8 metrics: challengesRaised=7, stressTestsRaised=4, challengesSurvived=1, challengesConceded=6, mitigationsHeld=3, mitigationsBroken=2, proposalModified=yes, confidenceScore=7

expectations.text: People impact summary with headcount changes, roles affected, transition support, attrition risk
expectations.evidence: People impact at lines 46-49: Headcount (+1 engineer, neutral CS), Roles affected (all 8 CSMs), Transition support (2-week training, monthly check-ins), Attrition risk (moderate for 1-2 CSMs)

expectations.text: Round 5 includes fallback plans for surviving challenges
expectations.evidence: Fallback plans: checkpoint model with escalation gates, front-loaded workshops, honest skills gap assessment with severance reserve, tightened unassisted metrics
